blogs.metaTitle: TikTok Shop US Setup for Non-Residents (2026)
blogs.metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to setting up TikTok Shop in the US as a non-resident. LLC requirements, EIN, verification, and seller tips for 2026.
blogs.content:
Why TikTok Shop Is the Biggest E-Commerce Opportunity in 2026

TikTok Shop has exploded from a novelty feature into a serious sales channel. In 2025, TikTok Shop generated billions in US gross merchandise value, and 2026 is shaping up to be even bigger. The platform's algorithm-driven discovery engine means a single viral video can generate thousands of sales overnight — something that takes months of optimization on Amazon or Shopify.

For international entrepreneurs, the opportunity is massive. But there is a catch: selling on TikTok Shop in the US requires a US business entity. You cannot simply sign up with a foreign passport and start listing products. TikTok enforces strict identity verification, business documentation checks, and banking requirements.

The good news? With the right setup, non-residents can absolutely sell on TikTok Shop from abroad. This guide walks you through every step, from LLC formation to your first product listing.

The US LLC Requirement: Why You Need One

TikTok Shop's US marketplace requires sellers to register as either an individual with a US Social Security Number (SSN) or as a US-registered business entity. Since non-residents typically do not have an SSN, the business entity path is the way forward.

A US Limited Liability Company (LLC) is the preferred structure for several reasons:

  • Legal separation — Your personal assets are protected from business liabilities
  • Tax flexibility — Single-member LLCs owned by non-resident aliens can benefit from favorable tax treatment on non-US-sourced income
  • Banking access — US banks require a US business entity to open a business account
  • Platform compliance — TikTok, Amazon, Walmart, and Shopify all accept US LLCs from foreign owners

We recommend forming your LLC in Wyoming or New Mexico, as both states are friendly to non-resident owners, have no state income tax on out-of-state income, and offer strong privacy protections. Our LLC formation service handles the entire process for you.

Step-by-Step: From Zero to TikTok Shop Seller

Here is the complete roadmap. Each step builds on the previous one, so follow them in order.

Step 1: Form Your US LLC

This is the foundation. You need Articles of Organization filed with a US state, a registered agent in that state, and an Operating Agreement. The process typically takes 3–7 business days depending on the state.

Critical detail: the legal name on your Articles of Organization must exactly match what you later enter in TikTok's Seller Center, on your EIN documentation, and on your bank account. Even a small discrepancy (like "LLC" vs. "L.L.C.") can trigger a manual review that delays your application by weeks.

Start your LLC formation here — we ensure all your documents are consistent and verification-ready.

Step 2: Get Your EIN (Employer Identification Number)

Your EIN is essentially your business's tax ID number, issued by the IRS. TikTok Shop requires it during seller verification. You will receive an IRS Notice CP 575 confirming your EIN, and this is one of the documents TikTok accepts as proof of your business identity.

For non-residents without an SSN or ITIN, the EIN application is done by fax or mail (Form SS-4), which typically takes 4–6 weeks through the IRS. Our EIN application service handles the paperwork and follow-up so you do not have to navigate IRS phone lines.

Step 3: Open a US Business Bank Account

TikTok Shop deposits your earnings into a US bank account. You need one. TikTok also cross-references your banking details against your LLC name and EIN, so everything must match.

Popular options for non-resident LLC owners include:

  • Mercury — Popular with startups and e-commerce sellers; remote-friendly application
  • Relay — Free business banking with no minimum balance
  • Bluevine — Offers checking with interest

Our business banking assistance guides you through the application and ensures your documents are in order before you apply.

Step 4: Apply for an ITIN (If Needed)

While an EIN is for your business, TikTok may also require a personal tax identification number for the Primary Business Representative. If you do not have an SSN, you will need an ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number). This is used for the W-9 form that TikTok requires for tax reporting.

Step 5: Register on TikTok Seller Center

Now for the actual TikTok Shop registration. Go to seller-us.tiktok.com and select "Sign Up" as a business seller. You will need:

  • Your LLC's legal name (exactly as on your Articles of Organization)
  • Your EIN and IRS CP 575 notice
  • A US business address (your registered agent address or virtual address works)
  • US bank account details
  • A W-9 form

TikTok cross-checks three independent data points: your entity name on the Articles of Organization, the EIN on your CP 575, and the legal name on your bank account. All three must match exactly.

Step 6: Appoint a Primary Business Representative

This is the step that trips up most non-resident sellers. Every TikTok Shop needs a US-based Primary Business Representative (PBR) — a person authorized to act on behalf of the company. The PBR must provide:

  • A US government-issued photo ID (driver's license or state ID)
  • A Social Security Number
  • A live selfie video for identity verification
  • A US residential address (not a PO Box)

If you are the LLC owner but live abroad, you will need to appoint someone in the US as your PBR. This is where the Letter of Authorization (LOA) comes in.

Step 7: Prepare Your Letter of Authorization

A Letter of Authorization is a written document confirming that a person of authority (you, the LLC owner) has granted permission for another person (the PBR) to act on behalf of your business on TikTok Shop. TikTok may request this document during verification.

Your LOA should include:

  • Your LLC's full legal name and EIN
  • The name and role of the person being authorized
  • A clear statement of what they are authorized to do
  • The LLC owner's or manager's signature
  • Date of authorization

TikTok sometimes provides a template for this. If they do not, a professionally formatted letter on your company letterhead works.

Identity Verification: What to Expect

TikTok's verification process has become stricter in 2026. The platform verifies your business at multiple levels:

  1. Business Entity Verification — Confirming your LLC is real and active with your state
  2. Tax Identity Verification — Matching your EIN and W-9 information
  3. Primary Business Representative Verification — ID check, SSN verification, and live selfie
  4. Beneficial Ownership Disclosure — Any owner with 25% or more ownership must be disclosed
  5. Bank Account Verification — Confirming the account matches your business name

Typical approval time is 24–48 hours if all documents are clean and consistent. Some sellers report approval in as little as 19 hours.

Common Rejection Reasons (and How to Avoid Them)

Understanding why applications get rejected will save you weeks of frustration. Here are the most common issues:

Rejection Reason What It Means How to Fix It
Business information inconsistent Name or address mismatch between documents Ensure LLC name is identical on all documents
Uploaded documents unclear Scans were blurry, cropped, or low resolution Upload high-resolution, full-page scans
ID information does not match PBR's name or date of birth entered incorrectly Double-check all personal details against the ID
PBR address mismatch PBR's ID address does not match portal entry Use the exact address shown on the PBR's ID
Bank account name mismatch Bank account name differs from LLC legal name Open bank account using exact LLC legal name

The golden rule: consistency is everything. Before submitting your application, lay out your Articles of Organization, CP 575 letter, bank statement, and W-9 side by side. The business name, address, and EIN should be identical across all of them.

Fulfillment Options: FBT vs. Third-Party Logistics

Once approved, you need to figure out how to actually get products to customers. As a non-resident seller, you have two main options.

Fulfilled by TikTok (FBT)

FBT is TikTok's in-house fulfillment service, similar to Amazon's FBA. You ship inventory to TikTok's fulfillment centers, and they handle storage, picking, packing, and shipping.

  • Pros: Faster delivery (3-day delivery badge boosts visibility), 20–35% shipping savings, 60 days free storage, reduced operational burden
  • Cons: Less control over packaging and branding, storage fees increase after 60 days, limited value-added services
  • Costs: Starting at $3.58 per single-unit order and $2.86 per item for multi-unit orders (as of January 2026)

Third-Party Logistics (3PL)

A 3PL warehouse receives, stores, and ships your products on your behalf. Popular 3PLs for TikTok Shop sellers include ShipBob, ShipHero, and Deliverr.

  • Pros: Custom packaging and inserts, multi-channel fulfillment (sell on TikTok, Amazon, and Shopify from one warehouse), more flexible storage terms, often lower long-term storage costs
  • Cons: No "Free 3-Day Delivery" badge, may have higher per-order costs for small volumes, requires more setup

For sellers just starting out, FBT is the easier choice. The free 3-day delivery badge significantly improves your product's visibility in TikTok's algorithm. As your business grows and you want more control over the customer experience, transitioning to a 3PL gives you more flexibility.

Product Listing Tips for TikTok Shop Success

Getting approved is half the battle. Here are practical tips to make your products actually sell.

Optimize for Discovery

  • Use keyword-rich product titles that match what shoppers search for on TikTok
  • Write detailed product descriptions with benefits, not just features
  • Upload at least 5 high-quality product images with white backgrounds and lifestyle shots
  • Add a product video (even a simple 15-second demo) — listings with videos get significantly more engagement

Price Competitively

TikTok shoppers expect deals. Factor in TikTok's 6% referral fee (3% for your first 30 days), fulfillment costs, and your product cost to set a price that is competitive while still profitable. The sweet spot for TikTok Shop products tends to be $15–$50.

Leverage Creator Partnerships

TikTok Shop's affiliate program lets creators promote your products in their videos. You set a commission rate, and creators who drive sales earn that commission. This is one of the most powerful features of TikTok Shop and costs you nothing unless a sale is made.

TikTok Shop Seller Fees at a Glance

Fee Type Rate Notes
Referral fee (most categories) 6% 3% promotional rate for first 30 days
Referral fee (jewelry/collectibles) 5% Category-specific rate
FBT fulfillment (single unit) From $3.58 Varies by weight tier
FBT fulfillment (multi-unit) From $2.86/item Up to 24% discount on multi-unit orders
FBT storage (first 60 days) Free Fees apply after 60 days
Affiliate commission You set the rate Typically 10–20% for creator partnerships

The Numbers That Should Excite You

TikTok Shop is still in its growth phase in the US, which means less competition and more algorithmic support for new sellers compared to saturated marketplaces like Amazon. Consider these facts:

  • TikTok has over 170 million US users, and shopping features are integrated directly into the For You feed
  • The average TikTok user spends 95 minutes per day on the app — that is 95 minutes of potential product discovery
  • New sellers get a promotional 3% referral rate for their first 30 days, saving you half the standard fee
  • Products with the FBT 3-day delivery badge see measurably higher conversion rates

How LLCPad Makes This Entire Process Simple

Setting up a TikTok Shop from abroad involves multiple moving pieces: LLC formation, EIN, banking, ITIN, and then the TikTok application itself. At LLCPad, we have helped hundreds of international entrepreneurs launch on US e-commerce platforms, and we offer a streamlined path:

  • LLC Formation — We file your LLC and ensure all documents are consistent for platform verification
  • EIN Application — We handle the IRS paperwork and get your CP 575 notice
  • Registered Agent — A US address for your LLC's legal correspondence
  • Virtual Address — A US business address for your TikTok Shop profile
  • Business Banking — We guide you through opening a US bank account remotely
  • ITIN Application — For personal tax ID when you do not have an SSN
  • TikTok Shop Setup — We walk you through the entire seller registration and verification process

Ready to Start Selling?

TikTok Shop represents a rare window of opportunity for international sellers willing to do the setup work. The platform is actively recruiting sellers, offering promotional fee rates, and investing heavily in its fulfillment infrastructure. The sellers who get in now, while the marketplace is still growing, will have a significant advantage over those who wait.

The setup process is not trivial — it requires a real US business entity, proper documentation, and attention to detail during verification. But with the right guidance, it is absolutely achievable from anywhere in the world.

Start your LLC formation today and take the first step toward building your TikTok Shop business.
